summary: The library has several real correctness gaps in the MPA and provider paths that would materially skew audits: soft-200 probing in `lib/probe.js`, broken browser-mode cookie injection in `lib/browser.js`, and a new `--via brightdata` adapter that cannot preserve asset bytes or content types. The crawl/anatomy/manifest pipeline also drops URL graph structure, so the canvas-engine resolver cannot reconstruct page relationships, and `sitepull_map` regeneration currently throws backend-chain data away. I did not find a live import of the deleted `lib/edit.js`, but `lib/serve.js` still carries dead

issues.description: The BrightData GET path hardcodes every upstream response to `content-type: text/html` and a UTF-8 text body, so `--via brightdata` will vendor HTML/error text in place of CSS/JS/images and lose the real response type entirely.
issues.suggested_fix: Pass through the provider's real headers and raw bytes for GETs, or restrict `--via` to top-level HTML fetches and fetch assets with a binary-safe direct fetcher.

issues.description: `isShell` only runs in SPA mode, so MPA mode treats any non-404 soft-200 as a real endpoint, which reproduces the DataDome-style false positives the audit notes call out for `/healthz`, `/.env`, `/sitemap.xml`, and similar paths.
issues.suggested_fix: Compute an MPA fallback fingerprint as well (status/body hash/length/content-type similarity to the root or clustered responses) and require a materially distinct response before marking an endpoint real.

issues.description: Browser-mode `--cookie` is effectively broken because cookies are only stored on a custom `ctx._pendingCookies` property that is never read or applied before navigation.
issues.suggested_fix: Apply the parsed cookies to the Playwright context with `ctx.addCookies()` for the target origin, or set them as extra HTTP headers before the first request.

issues.description: `sitepull_map` regeneration hardcodes `probe: { real: [] }`, so rerunning the map strips all backend chains even when the original clone discovered endpoints.
issues.suggested_fix: Persist probe and crawl metadata in the clone output and reload it here instead of regenerating anatomy from an empty probe payload.

issues.description: The MPA crawl result is block-scoped to vendoring and discarded before `buildAnatomy()` runs, so map generation cannot receive page-to-page crawl structure or render a page graph.
issues.suggested_fix: Hoist `crawl` into outer scope, persist it alongside the audit output, and pass it into `buildAnatomy()` when `--map` is enabled.

issues.description: The BFS queue and `pages` records keep only `{url, depth}` plus page payload, so parent-child edges are never recorded and the crawl graph cannot be reconstructed later.
issues.suggested_fix: Store the source URL for each enqueued link and persist discovered edges separately so downstream consumers can build a route graph.

issues.description: The manifest schema is only a flat file inventory, with no mapping back to source URLs, routes, or page hierarchy, which creates schema drift against any resolver expecting URL-level structure.
issues.suggested_fix: Extend the manifest with route/source metadata such as page URL, output path, parent URL, and asset referrer so diffs and resolvers can work at the site-structure level.

issues.description: The path traversal guard uses `filePath.startsWith(slugRoot)`, which can be bypassed by sibling paths that share the same prefix on Windows (for example `..\slug2\secret` when the slug is `slug`).
issues.suggested_fix: Compare normalized absolute paths with `path.relative()` or require `filePath === slugRoot || filePath.startsWith(slugRoot + path.sep)` after resolving both paths.

issues.description: The BrightData adapter has no retry/backoff and no special handling for provider-side 429/5xx or JSON error payloads, so transient unlocker failures surface as opaque bad fetches and can be vendored as if they were page content.
issues.suggested_fix: Check `resp.ok`, parse provider error bodies, and retry transient 429/5xx responses with bounded exponential backoff before returning a failure.

issues.description: The custom dotenv loader and its comments are stale Phase-5 code: startup still reads `.env` for the removed Claude `/edit` path, but no remaining code in `serve.js` consumes those variables.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete the loader entirely or re-scope it to the specific env vars still used by the v2 canvas-engine proxy.

issues.description: The `storageStatePath` `try/catch` does not validate anything because plain assignment cannot throw, so unreadable or malformed storage-state files only fail later with a less actionable Playwright error.
issues.suggested_fix: Validate the path up front with an existence/readability check and surface a direct error before calling `browser.newContext()`.

strengths: `lib/audit.js` is cleanly phase-structured, which makes the main control flow easy to reason about.
`lib/serve.js` no longer appears to import the deleted `lib/edit.js`, so the Phase 6 removal was at least completed at the module-reference level.
The provider and browser fetchers already share a fetch-like response contract, which makes the current bugs fixable without redesigning the whole pipeline.
